message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1, fixes applied vs main). Venv: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
The file /tmp/pass1_confirm.json is a JSON array of 22 pass-1 findings (each: issue, title, file, sev, problem, suggested_fix).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #1 of 2 for finding INDEX 10 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_confirm.json.
Read that item (its issue number, problem, suggested_fix, file). Then VERIFY THE APPLIED FIX on the current branch: read the changed source AND its regression test (git diff vs main, or just read the file + tests), and where useful run a PoC against the venv to confirm the original problem is actually resolved.
Return: appropriate = the fix correctly addresses the root cause in a sound way (not a hack/incomplete patch/over-claim); complete = the fix fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would catch a regression. Set either false if not, and say exactly what is missing. Echo the issue number in 'issue'.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
{
  "issue": "111",
  "title": "Graph-orchestration gate decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail \u2014 the documented \u201cappend-only trail\u201d for the durable path is unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state",
  "file": "/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py",
  "sev": "MEDIUM",
  "problem": "There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE \u00a73 states the chained ProvenanceTrail \"is the spine\u2026 No step can be retroactively removed or reordered,\" and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions list \"the append-only trail.\" But the graph path's decisions are NEVER written into the chained Prov",
  "suggested_fix": "Bridge graph gate decisions into the chained trail: have make_gate_node (or a thin wrapper) accept a ProvenanceTrail-backed recorder and record each validated decision via trail.record('gate', ...) at apply time, so the durable path's approvals/rejections land in the same hash-chained, verify_records-checkable trail as the synchronous path. At minimum, reconcile the docs to stop calling the unchai"
}

1	"""Human-in-the-loop gate node — the interrupt/resume protocol (GOVERNANCE.md).
2	
3	:func:`make_gate_node` turns an engine :class:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gates.Gate` plus a
4	proposal builder into a LangGraph node that pauses the run at the gate (``interrupt``),
5	surfaces a proposal to the operator, and on resume validates and records the decision.
6	
7	The **gate-correlation guard** rejects a resumed decision aimed at a *different* gate than the
8	one currently parked — a stale or misrouted decision (e.g. delivered late through the
9	governance channel) must not be applied to whatever gate happens to be waiting. The guard's
10	expected gate is the gate the operator named in the resume value, compared against this node's
11	gate; auto-deriving it from the parked gate would make the guard always match and thus a no-op.
12	
13	Two further hardened behaviors, ported faithfully from the prior platform's gate node:
14	
15	- **Re-prompt on a bad resume (not raise).** ``interrupt()`` consumes and *persists* the
16	  resume value into the checkpoint *before* the node returns, so raising on a malformed or
17	  mis-correlated value would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever — the gate
18	  would be permanently bricked. Instead the node validates inside a loop and re-fires
19	  ``interrupt()`` (carrying the error) on a bad value, so the operator can re-submit and
20	  actually recover.
21	- **Terminal-decision idempotency.** An ``approved`` decision is terminal: re-entering an
22	  already-approved gate is a no-op (it does not re-prompt or re-record). ``rejected`` and
23	  ``deferred`` are deliberately NOT terminal, so a reject→revise→re-review loop re-fires the
24	  interrupt as intended.
25	
26	The node returns a partial state update appending one record to ``gate_decisions``; a study's
27	graph state should declare ``gate_decisions`` with a list/add reducer so records accumulate
28	across gates. The decision/recording validator (:func:`apply_gate_decision`) is pure and
29	tested offline; only the ``interrupt`` call requires LangGraph (the optional ``orchestration``
30	extra, imported lazily).
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
36	
37	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
38	
39	GateProposal = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], Mapping[str, Any]]
40	
41	_VALID_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved", "rejected", "deferred"})
42	
43	
44	def apply_gate_decision(gate: Gate, resumed: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
45	    """Validate a resumed operator decision and return the state update to record it.
46	
47	    Raises :class:`GateError` if the resume value is malformed, targets a different gate
48	    (correlation guard), or carries an unrecognized decision.
49	    """
50	    if not isinstance(resumed, Mapping):
51	        raise GateError(
52	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resume value must be a mapping, got {type(resumed).__name__}"
53	        )
54	    target = resumed.get("gate_id")
55	    if target != gate.name:
56	        raise GateError(
57	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resumed decision targets {target!r} — stale or misrouted; "
58	            "rejecting (gate-correlation guard)"
59	        )
60	    decision = str(resumed.get("decision", "")).strip().lower()
61	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
62	        raise GateError(
63	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: invalid decision {decision!r} "
64	            f"(expected one of {sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)})"
65	        )
66	    # A rejection/deferral with no rationale is governance-relevant; record a sentinel rather
67	    # than a blank (incl. whitespace-only) so the audit trail is never silently empty.
68	    rationale = str(resumed.get("rationale") or "").strip() or "No rationale provided."
69	    record = {"gate": gate.name, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
70	    return {"gate_decisions": [record]}
71	
72	
73	# Only an approval is terminal: a re-entered approved gate is a no-op, but a rejected/deferred
74	# gate must re-fire its interrupt so a revise→re-review loop can re-prompt the operator.
75	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved"})
76	
77	
78	def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
79	    """True if ``gate_name`` already holds a terminal (approved) decision in ``gate_decisions``."""
80	    for record in state.get("gate_decisions", []) or []:
81	        if record.get("gate") == gate_name and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS:
82	            return True
83	    return False
84	
85	
86	GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
87	
88	
89	def make_gate_node(
90	    gate: Gate,
91	    propose: GateProposal,
92	    *,
93	    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
94	) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
95	    """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
96	
97	    ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
98	    asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
99	    ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
100	
101	    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
102	    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
103	    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
104	    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
105	    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
106	    """
107	
108	    def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
109	        if already_decided(state, gate.name):
110	            return {}  # terminal decision exists — do not re-prompt or re-record
111	        try:
112	            from langgraph.types import interrupt
113	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
114	            raise RuntimeError(
115	                "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
116	            ) from exc
117	        proposal = dict(propose(state))
118	        current: dict[str, Any] = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal}
119	        # Validate inside a loop: on a bad/mis-correlated resume, re-fire interrupt (next
120	        # resume index) carrying the error rather than raising — a raise would strand the
121	        # bad value in the checkpoint and replay it forever, bricking the gate.
122	        while True:
123	            resumed = interrupt(current)
124	            try:
125	                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
126	            except GateError as exc:
127	                current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
128	                continue
129	            if recorder is not None:
130	                for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
131	                    recorder(rec)  # land the decision in the hash-chained provenance trail
132	            return update
133	
134	    return node
135

diff --git a/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py b/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py
index 88f3806..8f084ae 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py
@@ -83,14 +83,26 @@ def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
     return False
 
 
+GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
+
+
 def make_gate_node(
-    gate: Gate, propose: GateProposal
+    gate: Gate,
+    propose: GateProposal,
+    *,
+    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
 ) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
     """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
 
     ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
     asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
     ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
+
+    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
+    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
+    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
+    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
+    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
     """
 
     def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
@@ -110,8 +122,13 @@ def make_gate_node(
         while True:
             resumed = interrupt(current)
             try:
-                return apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
+                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
             except GateError as exc:
                 current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
+                continue
+            if recorder is not None:
+                for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
+                    recorder(rec)  # land the decision in the hash-chained provenance trail
+            return update
 
     return node

content.text: Now let me look at the regression tests and the provenance trail to verify the recorder bridges into a hash-chained trail.

content.text: Let me check the provenance API signatures to confirm the test's usage matches, then run the test against the venv.

content.content:
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return tuple(
209	            {
210	                "seq": e.seq,
211	                "kind": e.kind,
212	                "summary": e.summary,
213	                "payload": dict(e.payload),
214	                "timestamp": e.timestamp,
215	                "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
216	                "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
217	            }
218	            for e in self._entries
219	        )
220	
221	
222	def verify_records(
223	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
224	    *,
225	    secret: bytes | None = None,
226	) -> None:
227	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
228	
229	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
230	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
231	    """
232	    prev = _GENESIS
233	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
234	        if e.seq != i:
235	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
236	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
237	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
238	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
239	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
240	            raise ProvenanceError(
241	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
242	            )
243	        prev = e.entry_hash
244	
245	
246	def from_records(
247	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",

---lines 40-95---
  **approved** decision is terminal (re-entry is a no-op); a **rejected/deferred** gate
  re-prompts so a revise→re-review loop works.
- **Durable parking** — gate graphs must be compiled with the durable checkpointer
  (`compile_graph(..., requires_checkpointer=True)`); without one `interrupt` is a silent
  no-op and the gate would never pause. Resume must use the *same* checkpointer (keyed by
  `run_id` as `thread_id`).

**State-shape requirement for cloners:** gate decisions are returned as
`{"gate_decisions": [record]}` partial updates, so a study's graph state must declare
`gate_decisions` with a list/append reducer — otherwise each gate would overwrite the prior
one and the append-only trail would lose all but the last decision.

## 2. Pre-registration

Before any confirmatory test runs, the relevant hypotheses are:

1. made specific and typed (what is claimed, the test, the data, the decision rule),
2. content-hashed and committed,
3. **RFC-3161 timestamped** against a trusted timestamp authority.

This is honestly characterized as a **timestamped adaptive design**: methods are fixed
first; the data informs the questions; the questions are then locked and timestamped before
they are tested. It is not claimed to be "predictions registered before any data."

> **Implementation status (engine):** pre-registration is enforced by construction **in the
> confirmatory runners** (`adjudicate`, `discover_and_confirm`; ADR-0009). They verify, via a
> supplied `TimestampAuthority`, that a hypothesis's proof **binds its content and id** (the
> canonical digest of its decision-bearing fields), that the attested **timestamp is
> verifiable**, and that the lock **precedes confirmation** — a hand-set sentinel string, a
> post-lock content/id swap, a forged proof, or a lock dated at/after the ordering instant are
> all refused. (In adjudication the ordering instant is captured *before the baseline build*, so
> a claim-derived hypothesis must be locked before the baseline exists; in discovery a
> data-surfaced hypothesis is necessarily locked inside the runner, so there the *content*
> binding is the load-bearing guarantee — see ADR-0009.) The engine ships one real,
> data-sovereign authority (`LocalHmacAuthority`, on-box HMAC) and the Protocol so a study can
> plug an RFC-3161 TSA. Honest scope: the local authority is tamper-evidence *relative to an
> on-box secret*, not third-party non-repudiation. The lower-level confirm *primitives*
> (`confirm_whole_corpus`/`confirm_unit_level`) gate on the cheap presence predicate by default
> and take an optional `authority=` to opt into the full check; a study that confirms outside a
> runner must pass it (or call `require_preregistered` itself). `Hypothesis.locked` is only a
> presence predicate; `methodology.preregistration.require_preregistered` is the
> methodology-grade check.

## 3. Provenance

An **append-only** audit trail (W3C PROV-DM style) records every action before the next
executes. No step can be retroactively removed or reordered. This is the spine of
reproducibility and of the independence claim.

> **Implementation status (engine):** wired (ADR-0010). `assay_engine.provenance.ProvenanceTrail`
> is an append-only, hash-chained trail; the study runner (`assay_engine.pipeline.run_study`)
> records every action — ingest, blind baseline, each locked hypothesis (discovery *and*
> claim-derived), every gate decision, each verdict, the scorecard, the report — *before the next
> runs*, and verifies the chain before returning. There is no remove/edit/reorder API.
> **Integrity, honestly, in two tiers:** the default unkeyed chain is *tamper-evident against
> naive tampering and accidental corruption* (a single-entry edit, a reorder, or a deletion is

docs/hardening/PASS-1.md:183:- **Problem:** There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE §3 states the chained ProvenanceTrail "is the spine… No step can be retroactively removed or reordered," and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions list "the append-only trail." But the graph path's decisions are NEVER written into the chained ProvenanceTrail: apply_gate_decision takes no trail/recorder and returns a bare dict (PoC confirmed: {'gate_decisions': [{'gate':'review','decision':'rejected','rationale':'no good'}]}), and grep shows no bridge from gate_decisions into ProvenanceTrail (the as_recorder bridge at provenance.py:152 is wired only to the synchronous Gate.evaluate path, not the gatenode). The graph's 'append-only trail' has no prev_hash/entry_hash, no verify_records, and relies entirely on the study correctly declaring a list/add reducer (a documented foot-gun: lines 47-50 warn that a wrong reducer silently drops all but the last decision). So for the durable, cross-process governance path — the one production runs that park for an operator actually use — the most governance-relevant mutations (human approve/reject decisions) are recorded only as unchained, tamper-evident-free state that can be reordered or dropped without detection. The integrity guarantee GOVERNANCE §3 advertises does not hold for this path.

content.content:
entry point to the LangGraph ``StateGraph`` builder; the engine owns construction and
    attaching the durable ``checkpointer``. Returns the compiled graph.

    Set ``requires_checkpointer=True`` for any graph containing gate nodes: ``interrupt`` is a
    silent no-op without a checkpointer, so a gate graph compiled without one would never park
    for the operator. The engine fails loud rather than ship a silently-broken governance
    graph (audit #G3). The engine cannot introspect the study-supplied ``build`` callback for
    gate nodes, so this is the caller's responsibility: **any study wiring**
    :func:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode.make_gate_node` **must pass
    ``requires_checkpointer=True``** (see GOVERNANCE.md "Gate interrupt/resume protocol").
    """
    if requires_checkpointer and checkpointer is None:
        raise RuntimeError(
            "compile_graph(requires_checkpointer=True): a gate-bearing graph needs a durable "
            "checkpointer — interrupt() cannot park the run for the operator without one"
        )
    _require_langgraph()
    from langgraph.graph import StateGraph

---GOV 90-110---
> is an append-only, hash-chained trail; the study runner (`assay_engine.pipeline.run_study`)
> records every action — ingest, blind baseline, each locked hypothesis (discovery *and*
> claim-derived), every gate decision, each verdict, the scorecard, the report — *before the next
> runs*, and verifies the chain before returning. There is no remove/edit/reorder API.
> **Integrity, honestly, in two tiers:** the default unkeyed chain is *tamper-evident against
> naive tampering and accidental corruption* (a single-entry edit, a reorder, or a deletion is
> caught by `verify_records`) but is **not** forgery-proof — a party who controls the serialized
> bytes can edit a payload and recompute the whole SHA-256 chain. Pass a `secret=` and the chain
> is HMAC-keyed, so the head cannot be recomputed without the secret (a downstream store cannot
> silently rewrite and re-seal history); `verify_records` must be given the same secret. An
> in-memory trail is still as trustworthy as its process, and non-repudiable third-party
> attestation of the trail's *time* is the same pluggable concern as pre-registration (§2),
> out of scope here. A caller may pass its own trail to `run_study` so a run that *raises* (a
> blocked gate, a firewall violation) still leaves an auditable partial trail.

## 4. Single-operator structural independence

Independence from operator bias is achieved structurally, not by a co-reviewer:

- **Code gates** the operator cannot silently bypass.
- **Append-only provenance** that records the full decision history.
- **Timestamped pre-registration** committing to hypotheses before they are tested.
- **A complete reproducibility package** published with findings.

content.text:
I now have everything needed to render a verdict.

**Assessment:**

The fix (`make_gate_node` gaining an optional `recorder: GateRecorder | None` keyword) is the *stronger* of the two suggested remedies — it provides a real bridge from the durable graph path into the hash-chained `ProvenanceTrail`, and the regression test (`test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail`) proves a bridged decision lands as a `kind="gate"` entry and that `verify_records(trail.entries)` confirms an intact chain. The test passes against the venv. The mechanism is sound: it reuses the existing chained trail and `record()` API, records at apply-time only on validated decisions, and iterates `update["gate_decisions"]`.

**Appropriate: yes** — root cause (no path from graph decisions into the chained trail) is addressed in a sound, non-hacky way that reuses the real provenance API.

**Complete: no** — two gaps:

1. **The recorder is optional and unwired with no enforcement.** The finding's core complaint is that "the durable, cross-process governance path — the one production runs actually use — records the most governance-relevant mutations as unchained state." The fix makes chaining *possible* but *opt-in*: a study that calls `make_gate_node(gate, propose)` with no `recorder` still gets exactly the old behavior — bare `gate_decisions` dicts, no chain. Unlike the sibling `compile_graph(requires_checkpointer=True)` guard (graph.py:60-64) which fails loud when a gate-bearing graph lacks a checkpointer, there is no equivalent guard/warning forcing or even nudging a recorder. So by default the integrity gap the finding describes still exists.

2. **The documentation overstatement was not reconciled.** The suggested fix's explicit "at minimum" was to stop calling the unchained `gate_decisions` list an "append-only trail" with §3-equivalent integrity, and to document that the graph path requires an explicit provenance bridge. `git diff main -- docs/GOVERNANCE.md` is empty: GOVERNANCE.md line 50 still calls the unchained list "the append-only trail," graph.py's gate-wiring warning (line 56-59) still mentions only the checkpointer (not the recorder), and ADR-0010 / GOVERNANCE never mention the `recorder` bridge. A cloner reading the docs gets no signal that they must pass a recorder for the durable path to be tamper-evident.

The new code-level docstring on `make_gate_node` (lines 102-106) does explain the recorder, which is good, but the higher-level governance docs the finding called out remain unchanged and still over-claim.

input.reasoning:
APPROPRIATE: yes. The fix adds an optional `recorder: GateRecorder | None` kwarg to make_gate_node (gatenode.py:86-134) that, when supplied, replays each validated decision into a real ProvenanceTrail via trail.record('gate', ...). This is the stronger of the two suggested remedies and addresses the root cause in a sound, non-hacky way: it reuses the existing hash-chained trail and its record() API (provenance.py:116), records only validated decisions at apply-time inside the loop, and iterates update['gate_decisions']. A regression test (tests/test_orchestration_graph.py::test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail) drives a recorder, asserts a kind=='gate' entry with the right payload, and calls verify_records(trail.entries) to confirm the chain is intact. I ran it against the venv: 4 related gate-node tests pass.

COMPLETE: no. Two gaps remain.

(1) The recorder is OPTIONAL and entirely UNWIRED, with no enforcement. The finding's core complaint is that the durable, cross-process governance path (the one production runs that park for an operator actually use) records human approve/reject decisions only as unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state. The fix makes chaining POSSIBLE but OPT-IN: make_gate_node(gate, propose) with no recorder yields exactly the old behavior — bare gate_decisions dicts, no prev_hash/entry_hash, no verify_records coverage. There is no guard/warning forcing or nudging a recorder, in contrast to the sibling compile_graph(requires_checkpointer=True) guard (graph.py:60-64) that fails loud for an unconfigured gate graph. grep confirms there are zero callers passing a recorder anywhere in src/. So in the default/documented wiring the integrity gap the finding describes still exists.

(2) The documentation overstatement the finding's 'at minimum' clause called out was NOT reconciled. `git diff main -- docs/GOVERNANCE.md` is empty. GOVERNANCE.md line 50 still calls the unchained gate_decisions list 'the append-only trail', conflating it with the §3 hash-chained spine ('No step can be retroactively removed or reordered'). graph.py's gate-wiring warning (lines 56-59) still mentions only the checkpointer, not the recorder. Neither GOVERNANCE.md nor ADR-0010 mentions the recorder bridge, so a cloner gets no signal that the durable path needs an explicit provenance bridge to be tamper-evident.

What's missing to be complete: either (a) wire/enforce a recorder so the durable path is chained by default (e.g., a require_recorder guard analogous to requires_checkpointer, or default-bind a trail), AND/OR (b) update GOVERNANCE.md line ~50 to stop calling the unchained list an 'append-only trail' with §3 integrity and document that the graph path requires an explicit provenance bridge for tamper-evidence.
input.evidence: gatenode.py diff vs main adds GateRecorder type + recorder kwarg; node() loop (lines 122-132) now captures update, calls recorder(rec) for each gate_decisions record, then returns. Regression test tests/test_orchestration_graph.py::test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail builds a recorder=lambda r: trail.record('gate', ...), runs node, then verify_records(trail.entries) and asserts gates[0].payload['decision']=='approved'. Venv run: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_orchestration_graph.py -k 'bridges or interrupts or reprompts'` -> 4 passed. Provenance API (provenance.py:116 record, 222 verify_records) matches test usage. Gaps: `git diff main -- docs/GOVERNANCE.md` empty (line 50 still says '...the append-only trail would lose all but the last decision' for the unchained list); grep 'recorder|bridge' over docs/GOVERNANCE.md, graph.py, ADR-0010 returns nothing; grep make_gate_node over src/ shows no caller passing recorder. No guard analogous to compile_graph requires_checkpointer (graph.py:60-64) for the recorder.

content.text:
Verdict recorded for issue 111: appropriate=true, complete=false.

The fix (optional `recorder` bridge in `make_gate_node`) correctly and soundly addresses the root cause and ships a passing regression test that proves bridged decisions enter an intact hash-chained trail. But it is incomplete: the recorder is opt-in and unwired (no caller passes it, no guard enforces it, unlike the sibling `requires_checkpointer` guard), so the default durable path still records decisions as unchained state; and the documentation overstatement the finding's "at minimum" clause called out (GOVERNANCE.md line ~50 calling the unchained `gate_decisions` list "the append-only trail") was not reconciled.
